title: TC001-Place an order successfully from menu to QR confirmation
description: Verifies a guest can add an item to cart, proceed to checkout, place an order, and see the QR confirmation page.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /susushi/menu?table=1 (use explicit navigate as test step specifies).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/susushi/menu?table=1")
        
        # -> Click 'Add to cart' on the first visible menu item (Spicy Edamame) using element index 718.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/main/div/div/div/div[2]/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Orders/Checkout link to proceed to the checkout page (click element index 1345).
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/nav/div/div/div/a[2]').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Proceed to Checkout' button (element index 1498) to navigate to the checkout page, then verify the URL contains '/susushi/checkout'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Proceed to Checkout' button on the Cart page to navigate to the checkout page and then verify the URL contains '/susushi/checkout'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the Confirm Order (Place order) button (element index 1543) to place the order and then verify the URL contains '/susushi/qr'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Menu')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Menu' to be visible"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Added')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Added' to be visible"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/susushi/checkout' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/susushi/qr' in current_url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- ASSERTION: Order confirmation failed - after clicking 'Confirm Order' the application displayed a toast 'No table selected. Please go back and select a table.' and did not navigate to '/susushi/qr'.
- ASSERTION: Checkout did not complete - current URL remained '/susushi/checkout' after attempting to place the order.
- ASSERTION: Add-to-cart feedback not found - the expected 'Added' confirmation text/toast was not visible after adding the item, although the cart badge indicates 1 item.

title: TC007-Admin can sign in with valid credentials and reach the Admin dashboard
description: Verifies that an admin can log in using email/password and is redirected to the /admin dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:3000
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (http://localhost:3000/login) as the explicit test step requires.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
        
        # -> Type the admin email and password into the form and click the 'Sign In' button.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('admin@susushi.com')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Input text
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/div[2]/div/input').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('amrasd12345')
        
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # -> Click the 'Sign In' button again (index 612) to retry login; after the click, verify the URL contains '/admin' and that the page displays the text 'Admin'.
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        # Click element
        elem = frame.locator('xpath=/html/body/div[2]/div/div[2]/form/button').nth(0)
        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sign in')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Sign in' to be visible"
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/admin' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Admin')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Admin' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- Login did not redirect to /admin; current URL is 'http://localhost:3000/login'.
- Sign-in form remains visible with email 'admin@susushi.com' and a non-empty password field.
- Two sign-in attempts were performed but no redirect to the admin dashboard occurred.
